frameworks.name: Meta Andromeda Algorithm Framework
frameworks.description: Meta's 2026 algorithm prioritizes creative diversity over targeting precision. Feed the system multiple creative variations and let AI find winning combinations.
frameworks.when_to_use: All Meta campaigns in 2026+. Essential for accounts spending $10k+ monthly.
steps: Upload 5-10 creative variations per ad set
Use Flexible Ad Format for automatic mixing
Let algorithm run 7-14 days before optimization
Analyze winning combinations via performance data
Scale winners, kill losers at creative level
frameworks.example: E-commerce brand uploads 8 video variations (different hooks, CTAs, formats). Meta finds 3 winning combinations driving 40% lower CPA than single creative approach.

frameworks.name: Performance Max Asset Hierarchy
frameworks.description: Google's Performance Max requires strategic asset organization across all inventory. Quality and quantity of assets directly impact reach and performance.
frameworks.when_to_use: Google Ads campaigns targeting multiple touchpoints. Mandatory for accounts with diverse product catalogs.
frameworks.example: SaaS company with complete asset library sees 67% higher impression share vs. minimal asset campaigns.

frameworks.name: Vertical-First Creative Strategy
frameworks.description: 90% of Meta inventory is vertical in 2026. Design for 9:16 first, adapt to other formats second.
frameworks.when_to_use: All social media advertising. Critical for mobile-first audiences.
steps: Design all creatives in 9:16 format first
Ensure key message visible in top 75% of frame
Test horizontal versions as secondary priority
Use Flexible Ad Format for automatic optimization
frameworks.example: Fashion brand redesigning for vertical-first sees 34% improvement in CPM efficiency across all placements.

key_principles.explanation: Meta's algorithm interprets your inputs literally. Vague targeting or creative direction produces vague results.
key_principles.application: Use specific, descriptive ad copy. If targeting 'entrepreneurs,' specify 'SaaS founders with 10-50 employees' instead.

key_principles.explanation: 2026 algorithms prioritize creative variety over narrow audience targeting. Feed multiple creative options, let AI optimize.
key_principles.application: Create 5-10 creative variations per campaign. Focus budget on creative production, not audience research.

key_principles.explanation: iOS 14.5+ requires server-side tracking. Conversion API captures 20-30% more conversions than pixel alone.
key_principles.application: Implement Conversion API for all Meta campaigns. Use it to pass offline conversions, email opens, and CRM data.

key_principles.explanation: Fragmented budgets limit algorithm learning. Consolidate spend into fewer, higher-budget campaigns.
key_principles.application: Run 1-3 campaigns with $100+ daily budgets instead of 10 campaigns with $20 budgets.

tactics.description: Upload top-performing ads to AI tools (Gemini, ChatGPT) with performance data. Analyze why they worked, generate new concepts based on insights.
Use Meta's Flexible Ad Format to automatically mix creative elements. Functions as built-in multivariate testing.
Split Performance Max by ROAS targets and product categories. One campaign per distinct business goal.
Create lookalike audiences from multiple high-value customer segments. Stack 1%, 2%, and 5% lookalikes in single ad set.
Increase budgets 30-50% during peak seasons, decrease during low periods. Use historical data to predict demand curves.

templates.name: Meta Ad Copy Formula
templates.template: Hook: [Specific pain point/desire] + Social proof: [Number/result] + Solution: [Clear benefit] + CTA: [Action verb + urgency]

templates.name: Performance Max Asset Checklist
templates.template: Headlines: [15 variations, 30 chars] | Descriptions: [5 variations, 90 chars] | Images: [20+ in 1.91:1, 1:1, 4:5] | Videos: [5+ vertical/horizontal] | Extensions: [All relevant types]

templates.name: Creative Brief Template
templates.template: Objective: [Specific goal] | Audience: [Detailed persona] | Key message: [Single benefit] | Proof point: [Data/testimonial] | Format: [9:16 primary] | CTA: [Specific action]

templates.name: Campaign Structure Template
templates.template: Campaign: [Product/service category] | Budget: [$X daily minimum] | Objective: [Specific conversion] | Audience: [Lookalike % + interests] | Creative: [5-10 variations]

common_mistakes: Over-targeting audiences in 2026 - let the algorithm find your customers
Using single creative per ad set - algorithm needs variety to optimize
Micromanaging campaigns daily - allow 7-14 days for learning phase
Ignoring Conversion API setup - missing 20-30% of actual conversions
Designing horizontal-first - 90% of inventory is now vertical
Fragmenting budgets across too many campaigns - limits algorithm learning
Blaming the platform for poor creative performance - creative is 80% of success
Not implementing proper attribution tracking - can't optimize what you can't measure
pro_tips: Use AI tools to analyze your best-performing ads and generate new concepts based on winning patterns
Implement Conversion API to capture offline conversions and improve algorithm optimization
Create separate Performance Max campaigns for different ROAS targets - don't mix goals
Test creative concepts in organic social first, then promote winners with paid budget
Use audience signals as suggestions, not restrictions - guide the algorithm, don't constrain it
Monitor creative fatigue through frequency metrics - refresh when performance drops 20%+
Leverage seasonal demand patterns - increase budgets 30-50% during peak periods
Stack multiple lookalike percentages (1%, 2%, 5%) in single ad sets for broader reach
